title: Fyg Boras
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Character |type=New Republic |image= |name=Fyg Boras |homeworld=[[Vortex/Legends|Vortex]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'' |birth= |death= |species=[[Vor]] |gender= |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] **[[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]] *[[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|What I hoped was to discuss the next Chief of State.''"
"''I'm committed to Fyor Rodan, as you know.''"
"''I was hoping to change your mind.|Lando Calrissian negotiates with Fyg Boras|}} '''Fyg Boras''' was a [[Vor]] [[Senator/Legends|senator]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] who represented the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vortex/Legends|Vortex]] in the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]] in [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]]. During the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], Vortex was among the many worlds that [[Battle near Vortex|fell]] to the invaders. Eventually, the galactic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] was also [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|captured]], forcing Boras and the other surviving members of the New Republic to take refuge on the planet [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]]. When former [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]]-turned-businessman [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Smugglers' Alliance]] leader [[Talon Karrde]] arrived on Mon Calamari with relief supplies, the pair approached Boras and struck an arrangement with him. They would provide him with twenty-five tons of relief supplies that were to be distributed to the refugees; in return, Boras was to vote for an [[New Republic military appropriations bill|amendment]] authorizing the New Republic to purchase Calrissian's [[YVH 1]] [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]]. However, Boras decided to sell the supplies for his own profit rather than giving them freely. Knowing that he would not be reelected to the Senate while his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was under Yuuzhan Vong occupation, Boras chose to prioritize his future well being instead. On their next meeting, Calrissian and Karrde were able to blackmail the Vor senator into switching his vote for the [[New Republic Presidential election, 28 ABY|next]] [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] from [[Fyor Rodan]] to [[Cal Omas]] by producing a [[Hologram/Legends|holo-recording]] of their initial meeting with Boras. ==Biography== ===Yuuzhan Vong War=== {{Quote|We have the supplies, but no way to distribute them. What we thought we'd do is present you with twenty-five metric tons of supplies. You can distribute them to your constituents as you think best.''"
"''That's certainly… very generous. Twenty-five tons?''"
"''Twenty-five tons. Though there is a catch.|Lando Calrissian bribes Fyg Boras|}} [[File:Vortex-GerFF96.png|thumb|right|250px|Vortex, homeworld of Fyg Boras]] The [[Vor]] Fyg Boras was a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Senator/Legends|senator]] who represented his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Vortex/Legends|Vortex]] in the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]] sometime in the middle of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-wide conflict between the New Republic and the warlike [[Yuuzhan Vong]].{{CSWECite|I|86|Boras, Fyg}} In [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]],According to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|fall]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to [[27 ABY]]. during the war, Vortex was [[Battle near Vortex|captured]] by the Yuuzhan Vong, leaving many of its inhabitants as refugees.''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' Later on in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the galactic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] was also [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|captured]] by the Vong, causing Boras to flee the doomed capital and make his way to the planet [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]] along with the other surviving members of the New Republic government. Due to former [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Borsk Fey'lya]]'s recent [[Death/Legends|death]] during the attack on Coruscant, an [[New Republic Presidential election, 28 ABY|election]] for the next Chief of State was held. Boras was part of a faction of senators who supported [[Fyor Rodan]], a senator known for his dislike of the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]] headed by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. When former [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]—now running the [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]]-manufacturing corporation [[Tendrando Arms]]—and [[Smugglers' Alliance]] chief [[Talon Karrde]] arrived on the planet, they offered their services to Skywalker in making sure that [[Cal Omas]], a senator who supported [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] involvement in the war, would win the election. ===Election of a New Chief of State=== {{Quote|If I'm guilty of taking a bribe—which I'm not—then you're guilty of offering one! To release that holo would be to put your own head into the noose!|Fyg Boras to Lando Calrissian|}} The pair arranged a private meeting with Boras, as well as several senators from Rodan's faction. Calrissian and Karrde offered Boras twenty-five metric tons of refugee supplies to be given out to the Vor survivors on Mon Calamari. In return, they asked that the senator cast his vote for an amendment in an upcoming [[New Republic military appropriations bill|military appropriations bill]], which would see the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic Military]] purchase [[YVH 1]] battle [[Droid/Legends|droids]] from Tendrando Arms. Boras agreed to the request, and, with the support of other similarly bribed senators, the amendment was approved in a rapid manner. [[File:LandoCalrissian-TUF.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Lando Calrissian, who blackmailed Fyg Boras into changing his vote]] Having been given the supplies, however, Boras decided to sell them for his own profit. Thinking of his future, Boras realized that with his homeworld under Yuuzhan Vong occupation, and without a homeworld to vote for him, his chances of being reelected were slim. However, Boras did not anticipate that Karrde and Calrissian would bribe a large number of other senators and flood the market with their own relief supplies, which kept the price of commodities low. With this turn of events, Boras was barely making profits while paying the warehouses that stored his own stock of relief supplies. Shortly after the amendment was passed, Calrissian and Karrde met with Boras once more to convince him to throw his support for Omas. When Boras declined and reaffirmed his commitment to Rodan, Karrde showed him a [[Hologram/Legends|holo-recording]] of their initial meeting, which showed Boras accepting the bribe to vote for the amendment. Boras argued that if the holo was publicly revealed, Calrissian and Karrde would also be guilty of bribery. Calrissian informed the Vor senator that what they intended to show was the fact that the relief supplies were supposed to be distributed for free to the refugees, not sold. He also reminded Boras that if such an act was proven, it would be considered illegal. To prevent the holo from being circulated, Boras ultimately announced public support for Omas's bid for Chief of State. With a large number of Rodan supporters—whom Calrissian and Karrde bribed or blackmailed—changing their votes, Omas won the election. ==Personality and traits== Having lost his homeworld to the Yuuzhan Vong, and worried that he wouldn't be reelected as a senator, Boras grew corrupt and readily accepted a bribe from Lando Calrissian and Talon Karrde in order to secure his financial future. Even though Calrissian explained that the relief supplies they were giving the senator were intended for the Vor refugees on Mon Calamari, Boras felt no remorse in secretly selling the supplies instead. When Calrissian and Karrde flooded the local market with supplies, thus bringing the prices down, Boras was more concerned that his sale profits were barely breaking even with the costs of warehousing his own stock, as opposed to caring about the welfare of his people. Boras favored a type of [[cologne]] with a discreet [[Mint/Legends|mint]] scent. ==Behind the scenes== Fyg Boras is a character created by author [[Walter Jon Williams]] for the [[2002]] novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'', the fourteenth installment in ''[[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order]]'' series. ==Appearances== *''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'' {{1st}} *[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way (abridged audiobook)|''The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way'' abridged audiobook]] ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Fyg Boras |es=Fyg Boras }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Boras, Fyg}} [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:New Republic Senators]] [[Category:Vors]]
